items.text: Sync begins with root hash exchange between source and target for a specific shard.
Identical root hashes short-circuit: sync completes immediately with no node transfer.
Differing root hashes trigger a simultaneous tree walk that descends only into subtrees where hashes differ.
Missing content-addressed nodes are transferred from source to target (CN1).
Sync operates per-shard: each shard syncs independently (CN2).
Sync uses beamr's existing distribution connections (CN5).
Pull sync: the target initiates, sends its root hash, and receives missing nodes from the source.
Push sync: the source responds to pull requests by walking its tree and sending requested nodes.
Received nodes are applied to the target's local node store.
A sync failure (network partition, crash) does not corrupt either node's local state (CN6).
When both nodes have diverged (independent writes to the same key), sync performs a merge using the configured conflict resolution policy.
Divergent writes are never silently dropped — they are resolved or surfaced (CN4).
The merged result is committed to the target's tree as a new root hash.
Eventual consistency mode syncs at configurable intervals without blocking the write path.
Strong consistency mode waits for quorum (N/2+1) acknowledgments before returning from a write (CN3).
Sync topology is configurable: full-mesh, ring, or custom sync pairs.
Sync scheduler triggers periodic syncs based on the configured topology and interval.
Any topology converges to consistency given sufficient sync rounds.
